title: Communicable disease threats report, 28 February – 6 March 2026 , week 10
summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 28 February–6 March 2026  and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, Sars-CoV-2 variant classification, MERS, mass gathering monitoring, and chikungunya.
raw_content: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 28 February–6 March 2026  and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, Sars-CoV-2 variant classification, MERS, mass gathering monitoring, and chikungunya.

title: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 9, March 2026
disease: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 9, March 2026
summary: Respiratory virus trends across the EU/EEA continue to show early signs of decline.
raw_content: Respiratory virus trends across the EU/EEA continue to show early signs of decline.

title: Communicable disease threats report, 21 - 27 February 2026, week 9
summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 21 - 27 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, mass gathering monitoring, chikungunya, cholera, and swine flu.
raw_content: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 21 - 27 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, mass gathering monitoring, chikungunya, cholera, and swine flu.

title: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 8, February 2026
disease: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 8, February 2026
summary: Respiratory virus trends across the EU/EEA show early signs of decline. RSV activity remains high, with young children most affected. Influenza circulation remains widespread, but continues to decrease in most countries. SARS CoV 2 activity remains low.
raw_content: Respiratory virus trends across the EU/EEA show early signs of decline. RSV activity remains high, with young children most affected. Influenza circulation remains widespread, but continues to decrease in most countries. SARS CoV 2 activity remains low.

title: Communicable disease threats report, 14 - 20 February 2026, week 8
summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period  7 - 13 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, mass gathering monitoring, avian influenza, and mpox.
raw_content: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period  7 - 13 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, mass gathering monitoring, avian influenza, and mpox.

title: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 7, February 2026
disease: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 7, February 2026
summary: Respiratory virus activity remains elevated across the EU/EEA: influenza transmission is still widespread but decreasing, and RSV levels remain high, while SARS CoV 2 circulation remains low.
raw_content: Respiratory virus activity remains elevated across the EU/EEA: influenza transmission is still widespread but decreasing, and RSV levels remain high, while SARS CoV 2 circulation remains low.

title: Communicable disease threats report, 7 - 13 February 2026, week 7
summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period  7 - 13 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, mass gathering monitoring, avian influenza, chikungunya, dengue, Nipah virus disease, and measles.
raw_content: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period  7 - 13 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, mass gathering monitoring, avian influenza, chikungunya, dengue, Nipah virus disease, and measles.

title: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 6, January 2026
disease: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 6, January 2026
summary: Respiratory virus circulation is elevated across the EU/EEA: the circulation of influenza viruses is still high and RSV circulation is elevated and increasing. SARS-CoV-2 circulation continues to be low.
raw_content: Respiratory virus circulation is elevated across the EU/EEA: the circulation of influenza viruses is still high and RSV circulation is elevated and increasing. SARS-CoV-2 circulation continues to be low.

title: Communicable disease threats report, 31 January - 6 February 2026, week 6
disease: Communicable disease threats report, 31 January - 6 February 2026
summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period  31 January - 6 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, SARS-CoV-2 variant classification, Bacillus cereus toxin in infant formula, mass gathering monitoring, mpox, nipah virus, and Middle East respiratory virus syndrome.
raw_content: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period  31 January - 6 February 2026 and includes updates on respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA, SARS-CoV-2 variant classification, Bacillus cereus toxin in infant formula, mass gathering monitoring, mpox, nipah virus, and Middle East respiratory virus syndrome.

title: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 5, January 2026
disease: Weekly respiratory virus update, week 5, January 2026
summary: Respiratory virus circulation is elevated across the EU/EEA. The circulation of influenza viruses remains high with early signs of a new increase, RSV circulation is elevated and increasing, and SARS-CoV-2 circulation remains at low levels.
raw_content: Respiratory virus circulation is elevated across the EU/EEA. The circulation of influenza viruses remains high with early signs of a new increase, RSV circulation is elevated and increasing, and SARS-CoV-2 circulation remains at low levels.
